title: xript Mod Manifest
description: Declarative manifest for xript mods. Declares metadata, required capabilities, entry scripts, and UI fragment contributions.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for editor autocomplete.

xript.description: The xript specification version this mod targets.

extends.description: One or more base mod manifests to inherit from. Resolved and deep-merged before validation: maps key-merge, arrays append, scalars are child-wins, and duplicate ids are an error. Paths are filesystem-relative to this manifest. Resolution is transitive with cycle detection.

name.description: Machine-readable mod identifier. Used in namespacing and generated package names.

family.description: Optional grouping family for host-side organization (e.g. grouping addons in a nav rail). When absent, hosts fall back to name-prefix heuristics.

title.description: Human-readable display name for the mod.

description.description: A brief description of what this mod does.

author.description: The mod author's name or handle.

license.description: The mod's license — an SPDX identifier or a short label (e.g. "MIT", "Apache-2.0", "Proprietary").

capabilities.description: Capabilities this mod requires from the host application.

entry.description: Script entry point(s) relative to the mod root directory. The bare string/string[] form runs in script mode with no exports. The object form additionally declares named host-invokable exports.

fills.description: The canonical contribution surface: an object keyed by host slot id, each value an array of fill entries. The inner shape of a fill is governed by the target slot's 'accepts' type (a fragment, a code-renderer entry, a role map, an event handler). Each filled slot id must exist in the host manifest, and the mod must hold the slot's capability if it declares one.

fragments.description: DEPRECATED in favor of 'fills': a fragment is a fill of a fragment-format slot. Retained for back-compat; still validated, but emits a deprecation warning.

contributions.description: DEPRECATED in favor of 'fills': 'provides' becomes a fill of a role-type slot. Retained for back-compat; still validated, but emits a deprecation warning.

provides.description: Logical roles this mod provides. The host resolves a role to a providing addon and its logical-to-concrete fn map; xript never invokes the named fns.

fill.description: A single fill engaging a host slot. The inner shape is governed by the target slot's 'accepts' type and is not policed here — a fragment fill carries 'format'/'source', a code-renderer fill carries 'kind'/'entry', a role fill carries 'fns', an event/hook fill carries 'handler'. Validation checks only that the slot exists and the mod holds its capability.

providerRole.description: A logical role the mod provides, mapping logical method names to concrete export/global fn names the mod registers.

role.description: Logical role identifier. Lowercase-hyphen; shared vocabulary with discovery results.

fns.description: Logical method name to concrete export/global fn name. The host calls e.g. fns.query, never xript.

entryBlock.description: The richer entry form: a primary script plus optional named exports the host can invoke.

script.description: The primary entry script path relative to the mod root.

format.description: Execution format. 'script' (default) evaluates the entry as a classic script; 'module' evaluates it as an ES module whose top-level named exports become the mod's host-invokable exports.

exports.description: Named callables the entry script registers via xript.exports.register and the host invokes by name.

exportDef.description: A host-invokable export declared by the mod. In script mode, the entry registers a matching function via xript.exports.register; in module mode, a matching top-level named export satisfies this automatically.

description.description: What this export does. Shown in generated docs and editor tooltips.

returns.description: The return type. Omit for void exports.

capability.description: Capability required to invoke this export. If omitted, the export is always invokable. Must be one of the mod's declared capabilities.

typeRef.description: A reference to a type. Can be a primitive, a custom type name, an array type, or a union.

parameter.description: A parameter with a name, type, and optional description.

fragment.description: A UI fragment that targets a host-declared slot.

id.description: Unique fragment identifier within this mod.

slot.description: Target slot ID from the host application's manifest.

format.description: MIME type of the fragment content.

source.description: Fragment content: a file path relative to the mod root, or inline markup when 'inline' is true.

inline.description: When true, 'source' contains inline markup rather than a file path.

bindings.description: Data bindings that wire host state to data-bind attributes in the fragment.

handlers.description: DOM-event handlers that wire events on this fill to sandboxed script functions. Each entry pairs a selector, an event name, and a handler function name.

events.description: DEPRECATED alias for 'handlers': the array holds event HANDLERS, not events, and was renamed accordingly. Still accepted; if both are present, 'handlers' wins. Migrate by renaming this property to 'handlers'.

priority.description: Ordering priority within the target slot. Higher values render earlier. Ties broken alphabetically by fragment ID.

fragmentBinding.description: A named data binding that resolves a host data path to a local name for use in data-bind attributes.

name.description: Local binding name referenced by data-bind attributes in the fragment markup.

path.description: Dot-separated path to resolve against the host's data layer (e.g. 'player.health.val').

fragmentHandler.description: A declarative event-to-handler mapping. The runtime attaches event listeners matching the selector and delegates to the named handler in the mod's sandboxed script.

selector.description: CSS selector targeting element(s) within the fragment. For non-HTML formats, the targeting mechanism is format-specific.

on.description: DOM event name to listen for (e.g. 'click', 'input', 'change').

handler.description: Name of a function in the mod's sandboxed script to call when the event fires.
